chief_complaint: A 40-year-old woman presented to the emergency department with chest pain and pain in the joints of her hands and feet.
history: The patient reported a 2-day history of worsening chest pain, described as a dull ache, and pain in the joints of her hands and feet. She denied any recent trauma or injury. She has a history of hypertension and hyperlipidemia. She takes lisinopril and atorvastatin. She has a family history of heart disease. She is a non-smoker and does not drink alcohol.
exam: Vital signs: BP 140/90 mmHg, HR 90 bpm, RR 18/min, O2 sat 98% on room air. Physical examination revealed diffuse tenderness to palpation in the chest, hands, and feet. The patient had a 2+ pitting edema in both lower extremities.
labs: ECG: normal sinus rhythm with ST-segment depression in leads II, III, and aVF. Blood work: elevated troponin levels (0.5 ng/mL), normal creatine kinase levels, and a mild anemia (Hb 10.5 g/dL). Imaging: normal chest X-ray, but a Doppler ultrasound of the lower extremities revealed bilateral deep vein thrombosis.
correct_diagnosis: Acute Coronary Syndrome with Pulmonary Embolism
differentials: Acute Coronary Syndrome with Pulmonary Embolism
correct_ranking: Acute Coronary Syndrome with Pulmonary Embolism
